questions.stem: The microdeletion marked on the long arm of chromosome 22 in this figure is most classically associated with which clinical syndrome?

explanation.summary: A hemizygous deletion at 22q11.2 causes DiGeorge/velocardiofacial syndrome, featuring conotruncal cardiac defects, thymic and parathyroid hypoplasia, palatal anomalies, and characteristic facies.
explanation.whyCorrect: A hemizygous deletion at 22q11.2 causes DiGeorge/velocardiofacial syndrome, featuring conotruncal cardiac defects, thymic and parathyroid hypoplasia, palatal anomalies, and characteristic facies.

questions.stem: The events depicted here (moving a segment to a new location, losing a segment, gaining an extra copy, flipping a segment, and exchanging segments between non-homologous chromosomes) all belong to which single category of mutation?

explanation.summary: Insertion, deletion, duplication, inversion, and translocation all alter chromosome structure rather than chromosome number or a single base, so they are collectively classified as structural chromosomal aberrations.
explanation.whyCorrect: Insertion, deletion, duplication, inversion, and translocation all alter chromosome structure rather than chromosome number or a single base, so they are collectively classified as structural chromosomal aberrations.
